what_not_to_say: Do not provide a specific workflow before the participant's intent is clear.

Do not ask the participant to confirm Participant's name, Participant's email, Record Keeper, Participant Status, MFA status, or First contribution posted status when those should be retrieved from admin or portal systems for participant-specific/account-access guidance.
Do not continue with participant-specific guidance if no matching participant profile is found.
Do not say the participant can access the Beneficiaries option before First contribution posted status shows the first contribution has posted.
Do not tell the participant that archived statements, custom date account summaries, or statements from several years ago are available in the dashboard.
Do not tell the participant that on-demand statements can be generated directly from the dashboard.
Do not provide a specific Paper Mail delivery timeline.
Do not invent missing account details, statement availability, fee amounts, or plan rules.

participant_message_components: The Beneficiaries option becomes available once your first contribution has posted to your 401(k) account.
If your first contribution has not posted yet, the Beneficiaries option may not be accessible in the dashboard.
This does not mean you are permanently unable to add beneficiaries; it means the dashboard option may not be available yet based on the article.
You can still use the dashboard for other available self-service options, such as viewing available statements or managing statement delivery preferences, if you can log in.
next_steps: Confirm from admin or portal systems whether the participant's First contribution posted status shows a posted positive contribution before explaining why the Beneficiaries option may not be available.
If First contribution posted status shows the first contribution has not posted, explain that the Beneficiaries option becomes available once the first contribution has posted.
Do not ask the participant to confirm First contribution posted status; retrieve it from admin or portal systems.

For other needs such as viewing statements, downloading available statements, or changing statement delivery preferences, provide the applicable dashboard steps if supported by the participant's request.
If the participant cannot log in, cannot access the needed dashboard section, or cannot complete the self-service steps, direct them to ForUsAll Participant Support.

If the participant needs help managing beneficiaries after the option becomes available, direct them to the Beneficiaries option from the dashboard three-dot menu or to ForUsAll Participant Support if they cannot complete the task.
warnings: Do not tell the participant they can access the Beneficiaries option before First contribution posted status shows the first contribution has posted.
Do not claim the participant is permanently ineligible to add beneficiaries.
Do not guarantee timing for when the first contribution will post.

Do not use Participant Status, Record Keeper, MFA status, login issues, or missing statements as blocked_not_eligible outcomes; this outcome applies only when the Beneficiaries option is unavailable because the first contribution has not posted.
Do not block statement access or statement delivery preference guidance just because the Beneficiaries option is not yet available.
what_not_to_say: Do not add eligibility requirements not stated in the article.
Do not claim the participant is permanently ineligible to add beneficiaries.
Do not guarantee timing for when the first contribution will post.
Do not say the Beneficiaries option is available before First contribution posted status shows the first contribution has posted.
Do not ask the participant to confirm whether the first contribution has posted when First contribution posted status should be retrieved from admin or portal systems.
Do not tell the participant that all dashboard features are unavailable just because the Beneficiaries option is not available.

Do not redirect the participant to support before explaining the self-service rule when the issue is only that the Beneficiaries option is not visible because First contribution posted status shows the first contribution has not posted.

participant_message_components: If you are married and name someone other than your spouse as primary beneficiary, your plan may require spousal consent.
Because this can depend on plan rules, the article does not say that spousal consent is always required or never required.
The ForUsAll team can help guide you if spousal consent may apply.
You can still start from the Beneficiaries section in your dashboard once your first contribution has posted.
next_steps: Use this frame when the participant asks whether spousal consent is required or when the participant is married and wants to name someone other than their spouse as primary beneficiary.
Explain only that spousal consent may be required, because the article states this as plan-variable.
Do not make a definitive plan-specific conclusion unless the plan rule is confirmed from a supported source.
Direct the participant to ForUsAll Participant Support for guidance if spousal consent may apply.

If the participant can access the dashboard and First contribution posted status shows the first contribution has posted, also provide the self-service step to open the three-dot menu and select Beneficiaries.

If the Beneficiaries option is not visible, explain that the option becomes available once the first contribution has posted and use First contribution posted status from admin or portal systems when participant-specific guidance is needed.
warnings: Spousal consent is plan-variable according to the article.
The article says the plan may require spousal consent; it does not say spousal consent is always required.
The article does not say spousal consent is never required.
Do not provide plan-specific spousal consent rules unless confirmed from a supported source.
questions_to_ask: Are you married and naming someone other than your spouse as your primary beneficiary?
what_not_to_say: Do not guarantee that spousal consent is required.
Do not guarantee that spousal consent is not required.
Do not provide plan-specific spousal consent rules not stated in the article.
Do not tell the participant they can avoid spousal consent.
Do not tell the participant spousal consent will definitely be requested.
Do not treat spousal consent as a blocker for all beneficiary updates.
Do not say the Beneficiaries option is available before First contribution posted status shows the first contribution has posted.
